decisions.summary: Enforce live-code-path trace via a deterministic launcher-side validator, not just prompt language. Missing/empty traces cause the requirements launcher to emit REVIEWER_FAILED instead of REVIEWER_READY, so the synthesis agent never sees a 'passed' requirements report without traces.

Trace section format: a dedicated '## Live Code Path Traces' section after '## Findings' with one '### AC: <title>' block per AC the reviewer claimed Implemented or Partial. Each block MUST contain a `**File:**` line with a backticked `path:line` reference, a `**Function:**` line, and a free-text `**Path:**` describing how user input reaches the function. This shape is greppable by a small regex parser and is the contract bead 2 validates.

Validator only requires traces on ACs the reviewer marked Implemented or Partial AND classified as `Scope: in_pr_scope`. ACs marked Missing, Not applicable, or with whole_feature_scope / pre_existing scope do not require a trace — there is no live code path in this PR for the reviewer to point at, and forcing one would re-create the very pre-existing-noise problem PAN-1217 just fixed.

Validator lives as a pure TypeScript function (`src/lib/cloister/review-requirements-validator.ts`) plus a thin CLI shim `pan review validate-trace <file>` (exit 0 = pass, exit 1 with a one-line reason on stderr = fail). The cloister launcher composer wraps the requirements sub-role launcher so that on claude exit it runs the validator and signals REVIEWER_READY or REVIEWER_FAILED based on the validator outcome — never on file presence alone.

Validator wiring is scoped to the requirements sub-role only. Security/correctness/performance reviewers do not need AC traces (they don't review against ACs) and must continue to use the existing 'report file present → REVIEWER_READY' contract unchanged.

Out of scope (and explicitly out of scope per the issue): actually executing the cited code path to verify behavior. That belongs to the test role. The substrate fix here is only that the reviewer must NAME the code path; the test role will VERIFY it.

Failure reason from the validator must enumerate the AC titles missing a trace (e.g. 'requirements review missing live code path trace for ACs: AC-2, AC-5'). This propagates verbatim into the REVIEWER_FAILED signal, into the synthesis agent's blocking note, and into the feedback the work agent sees when review fails — closing the loop without an LLM judgment call in between.

hazards.summary: Format drift: the reviewer LLM may emit `Trace:` / `Path:` / `Evidence:` instead of the canonical `**File:** \`path:line\`` shape, causing every requirements review to fail validation.
hazards.mitigation: Bead 1 puts an explicit verbatim format example in the prompt (`**File:** \`src/foo/bar.ts:42\`` etc.) and forbids the reviewer from omitting the section. Bead 2's parser is intentionally strict about the `**File:**` token but lenient about trailing whitespace/casing inside the path. The fixture tests in bead 2 cover the exact-format and near-miss-format cases.

hazards.summary: Pre-existing or whole_feature_scope ACs have no live code path inside the PR diff. Demanding a trace for them would either force the reviewer to invent one (silent-miss pattern returns) or block every PR that touches a multi-PR feature.
hazards.mitigation: D3: validator only requires traces for ACs the reviewer marked `in_pr_scope` AND Implemented/Partial. Bead 1 explicitly tells the reviewer this exemption, and bead 2 includes a fixture where a whole_feature_scope AC has no trace and the validator passes.

hazards.summary: Launcher generator changes are easy to get subtly wrong — the existing `buildReviewSubRoleCommand` is shared by all four sub-roles, and a regression that broke security/correctness/performance signaling would block every review across the project.
hazards.mitigation: Bead 3 keeps the requirements path as an additive branch keyed on `subRole === 'requirements'`. Existing launcher-generator snapshot tests stay green for the other three sub-roles; new snapshot tests cover the requirements path. Bead 3 explicitly verifies the other sub-roles' generated scripts are byte-identical to the pre-change output.

hazards.summary: Validator regex on file paths could over-match (e.g. accept the word `path` on its own) or under-match (e.g. reject Windows-style paths that surface from an agent on a remote workspace).
hazards.mitigation: Parser requires the `**File:**` value to be inside backticks AND match the pattern `\S+\.[A-Za-z]+:\d+` (at least one extension and a colon-line-number). Fixtures in bead 2 cover Windows-style backslashes, missing extension, missing line number, multiple paths in one block, and the canonical happy path.

hazards.summary: On a retry cycle the reviewer might re-emit the same file with traces appended in a malformed place (e.g. inside a code fence) and the validator's failure reason would be opaque to the next reviewer instance.
hazards.mitigation: Validator failure reason names the offending AC titles (not just 'missing trace'), and bead 1's prompt tells the reviewer that if it sees a REVIEWER_FAILED reply citing missing traces it must add a Live Code Path Traces section in the exact format shown. Bead 4 e2e test exercises this round-trip.

hazards.summary: If `pan review validate-trace` is missing (e.g. an older panopticon-cli binary on a remote workspace), the launcher branch will fail in a way that silently signals REVIEWER_FAILED on every requirements run.
hazards.mitigation: Bead 3 launcher script checks for the subcommand's availability first (`command -v` style) and, if absent, falls back to the legacy file-present signal AND emits a one-line warning into the activity log so the operator can see the substrate is degraded. This is the only acceptable bandaid because the alternative is breaking every review on stale workers.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1498: Substrate fix (PAN-1454 pattern 1): require live-code-path trace in requirements review
Planning complete (--auto mode). 4 beads scoped: prompt extension (foundation), pure-function validator with fixture tests, CLI shim + launcher wiring, end-to-end signal round-trip test.
